work_package_result.summary: Replaced the unused M00 protocol scaffold with the provider-neutral M01 System, Project and Session epoch, generated deterministic Rust and TypeScript clients, fenced authoritative watches by Authority Epoch, and closed the explicit breaking-change gate with owner approval and detached R3 review.

passed: 13 focused protocol/codegen tests cover exact descriptors, migration hashes, pinned CI actions and negative contract mutations
protocol_codegen.py check passed strict Buf STANDARD lint, exact descriptor contracts, deterministic Rust/TypeScript generation, the hash-pinned epoch boundary and negative lint/breaking probes
mise exec -- just check passed repository, documentation, planning, Rust, Python, TypeScript and protocol gates
GitHub Fast Gate and Protocol Compatibility workflows passed for commit 1a5d084edf1424bc0e2c5806a8a6fd573122560b
git diff --check passed and the gross 9322-line change remained within the explicit 10000-line package budget
not_run: runtime IPC behavior is implemented and scenario-tested by later M01 work packages
no persisted-data migration rehearsal exists because this epoch removes only unused generated APIs

review.method: Detached adversarial R3 protocol review followed by closure re-review of commit 1a5d084edf1424bc0e2c5806a8a6fd573122560b; the final verdict was PASS.
findings_closed: Added Authority Epoch fencing and typed resync reasons to both system and session watch contracts
Added the canonical SystemService.Watch snapshot and delta stream
Separated durable command admission and stable identity allocation from authoritative completion
Made every unary response an explicit typed result-or-error envelope
Replaced source-text assertions with exact descriptor-level service, field, presence, oneof and streaming checks
Parsed the protocol workflow structurally and pinned its read-only token, action and version configuration
Bound handshake proof and client session identity to the trusted bridge, validated OS peer, installation, Authority Epoch and reconnect lifecycle

migrations: M00-SCAFFOLD-TO-M01 retires package dennett.v1 and introduces dennett.common.v1, dennett.control.v1 and dennett.sync.v1 under exact old/new module hashes
DEBT-0001 and DEBT-0002 are retired; no persisted user data changes
observability_changes: protocol diagnostics identify exact service modes, field numbers, presence, oneof membership and epoch-manifest drift
wire errors expose safe message keys, correlation IDs and opaque details handles without provider payloads or credentials
known_limitations: This package defines and generates the M01 wire contract but does not implement the desktop, Node bridge or Head services
Only the local desktop conversation surface is included; memory, voice, mobile, external effects and multi-device protocols remain with later owners
The one-time breaking exception applies only to the unused M00 scaffold; later protocol changes return to additive WIRE_JSON compatibility
